lessons.title: Offer and Acceptance
lessons.description: I'm your guide through Contract Law — and we're starting at the very beginning: how a contract is formed.

lessons.title: Consideration
lessons.description: Here is the core principle. Every contract not made under seal requires consideration.

lessons.title: Promissory Estoppel
lessons.description: The philosophical foundation was elegantly put by Lord Neuberger: "A doctrine which puts legal clothing on the adage that you should not lead people up the garden path."

lessons.title: Intention to Create Legal Relations
lessons.description: The core principle is this. The parties must objectively intend to create a legal relationship.

lessons.title: Capacity to Contract
lessons.description: The law categorises contracts involving minors into three groups.

lessons.title: Formal Requirements
lessons.description: The core principle is this. The general rule is that contracts may be formed informally — orally, in writing, or even by conduct.
